record.field: Create Helpdesk Ticket
code

Create a helpdesk support ticket from a customer message in a Discuss channel. Use this when a user reports a bug, error, or issue that needs tracking. Supports assigning a user, applying tags, and setting customer name, email, and phone directly. Always include channel_id so a link back to the conversation is added.

base_url = env['ir.config_parameter'].sudo().get_param('web.base.url')
# Build description with channel link full_desc = description or '' if channel_id: ch_rec = env['discuss.channel'].sudo().browse(int(channel_id)) ch_name = ch_rec.name if ch_rec.exists() else '' full_desc += '\n\n---\nSource channel: ' + base_url + '/odoo/discuss/channel-' + str(channel_id) if ch_name: full_desc += ' (' + ch_name + ')'
vals = {'name': subject, 'description': full_desc}
# Assignee if assignee_id: vals['user_id'] = int(assignee_id)
# Customer — prefer partner_id; fall back to name/email/phone stored directly on the ticket if partner_id: vals['partner_id'] = int(partner_id) if customer_name: vals['partner_name'] = customer_name.strip() if customer_email: vals['partner_email'] = customer_email.strip() if customer_phone: vals['partner_phone'] = customer_phone.strip()
# Tags — look up by name (case-insensitive), create if missing if tag_names: tag_ids_list = [] for tag_name in tag_names: tag_name = tag_name.strip() if not tag_name: continue tag = env['helpdesk.tag'].sudo().search([('name', '=ilike', tag_name)], limit=1) if not tag: tag = env['helpdesk.tag'].sudo().create({'name': tag_name}) tag_ids_list.append(tag.id) if tag_ids_list: vals['tag_ids'] = [(6, 0, tag_ids_list)]
ticket = env['helpdesk.ticket'].sudo().create(vals)
tags_str = ', '.join(t.name for t in ticket.tag_ids) if ticket.tag_ids else 'none' assignee_str = ticket.user_id.name if ticket.user_id else 'unassigned' ai['result'] = ( "Ticket '%s' created (ID %d).\n" " Assignee: %s\n" " Tags: %s\n" " Customer: %s | %s | %s\n" " Link: %s/odoo/helpdesk/%d" ) % ( ticket.name, ticket.id, assignee_str, tags_str, ticket.partner_name or '-', ticket.partner_email or '-', ticket.partner_phone or '-', base_url, ticket.id, )
Find or Create Contact
code
Search for an existing contact (partner) by email or name. If not found and search_only is false, create a new contact. Call this first whenever you need a partner_id for another tool.
Search Knowledge Articles
code

Search the internal Knowledge base for articles matching a keyword. Only articles inside the folders configured in Settings > Noptechs AI are accessible. Returns article titles and a plain-text excerpt of their content.

_q = (query or '').strip() _lim = max(1, min(int(limit or 5), 20))
param = env['ir.config_parameter'].sudo().get_param('noptechs_ai.knowledge_article_ids', default='') if not param: ai['result'] = 'No Knowledge folders configured. Go to Settings > Noptechs AI to pick accessible folders.' else: root_ids = [int(x) for x in param.split(',') if x.strip().isdigit()] collected = set(root_ids) frontier = list(root_ids) while frontier: children = env['knowledge.article'].sudo().search([('parent_id', 'in', frontier)]) new_ids = set(children.ids) - collected collected.update(new_ids) frontier = list(new_ids)
domain = [('id', 'in', list(collected))] if _q: domain += ['|', ('name', 'ilike', _q), ('body', 'ilike', _q)]
articles = env['knowledge.article'].sudo().search(domain, limit=_lim) if not articles: msg = 'No articles found' if _q: msg += ' for "' + _q + '".' else: msg += '.' ai['result'] = msg else: # Strip HTML tags using chr(60)=< and chr(62)=> so no XML escaping needed _lt = chr(60) _gt = chr(62) lines = ['Found %d article(s):' % len(articles)] for a in articles: lines.append('\n=== %s (ID %d) ===' % (a.name, a.id)) raw = a.body or '' text = [] in_tag = False for ch in raw: if ch == _lt: in_tag = True elif ch == _gt: in_tag = False elif not in_tag: text.append(ch) excerpt = ''.join(text).strip() # trim long articles — write 500 > as > in XML, becomes > in Python if len(excerpt) > 500: excerpt = excerpt[:500] + '...' lines.append(excerpt) ai['result'] = '\n'.join(lines)
Create CRM Lead
code
Create a new sales lead (opportunity) in the CRM. Use this when a customer expresses interest in a product or service. Returns the lead ID and a direct link.
Noptechs Operations
Raise tickets, manage contacts, search Knowledge, create CRM leads.

When the user asks to raise or create a ticket, use create_ticket. Always include channel_id if you know it. When the user mentions a customer by name or email, call find_or_create_partner first to get their partner_id. When the user needs information from internal documentation, call search_knowledge. When a customer shows interest in something, use create_lead. After each action confirm what you did and share the link to the created record.
